topic: RevDeck next powerful features and TUI optimization
strategy: Build user-visible TUI power navigation first, then connect it to real adapter execution and host-mediated plugin commits.

features.summary: Promote local relations into a first-class Graph Lab lens with current-object subgraphs, xref filters, relation direction, evidence paths, and jumpable rows.
Run local adapter plugins through the host, validate output, commit ObjectBatch facts transactionally, and record plugin contributions with provenance.
Add an overlay palette for commands, object search, recent targets, help, and mutation previews so users do not need to memorize command syntax.
Expand Binary Map with PE/ELF format details, sections, entropy signals, import grouping, resources, parse warnings, and packed/unknown-file guidance.
Turn radar leads, warnings, weak boundaries, and finding gaps into a review queue with states and one-step promotion into findings with evidence.
Let first-party or plugin rule packs emit explainable score reasons, confidence, suppressions, thresholds, and deterministic merge behavior.
Ship fixture-backed adapters for Ghidra/rizin JSON export, trace JSONL, ASAN/panic crash logs, and binwalk-style firmware trees.
Improve layout density, status chips, breadcrumbs, no-data panels, large-list paging, stable rendering, and small-terminal behavior.

exit_criteria: Graph Lab is a first-class workspace lens
Command Deck can search commands and objects with previews
Binary Map shows useful parse and packer diagnostics
Triage items can be reviewed and promoted into findings
TUI render/reducer tests cover desktop and small terminal layouts
Local adapter runner records audited plugin runs
Plugin contributions are queryable and reversible or hideable by source
At least one first-party adapter fixture imports external evidence
Rule packs emit structured score reasons
Scoring replay is deterministic across fixtures
